<!--
Thanks for the pull request! Fill in every section below, and delete a
section that does not apply rather than leaving it empty. Detailed guidance:
.claude/skills/development-guidelines/references/pull-request-descriptions.md

Title: use a Conventional Commits header — `<type>[scope][!]: <description>`
(e.g. `fix(parser): handle empty input`), per
.claude/skills/development-guidelines/references/commit-messages.md.

Keep the pull request small and single-purpose; split unrelated changes into
separate pull requests.
-->

## Summary

<!--
What this change does and, above all, WHY. The diff already shows what
changed — explain the problem or motivation, why this approach was chosen,
and any known trade-offs or shortcomings. Write it self-contained: linked
pages rot or sit behind access walls, so summarize their key points inline.
-->

## Related issues

<!--
Link the tracking issue with a closing keyword so merging closes it. Put
closing keywords only here in the body, never in commit messages. If there
is no related issue, say so here instead of deleting the section.
-->

Closes #

## Verification

<!--
Evidence, not assertion: the commands run and their observed results, manual
checks performed, and any required check that was skipped and why. When the
linked issue lists acceptance criteria, state each criterion's status. For
user-visible changes, add before/after screenshots or a recording.
-->

## Risks and breaking changes

<!--
Breaking changes (what breaks, who is affected, the migration path), risky
areas to scrutinize, and rollback notes. Delete this section if there are
none.
-->

## Notes for reviewers

<!--
Help the review start well: where to begin reading, what kind of feedback
you want, contentious decisions, and open questions. Delete this section if
there is nothing to flag.
-->
